    def reverseBits(self, n: int) -> int:

            reversed_num = (reversed_num << 1) | (n & 1)
            n = n >> 1
        return reversed_num

solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def reverseBits(self, n: int) -> int:
        reversed_num = 0
        for _ in range(32):
            reversed_num = (reversed_num << 1) | (n & 1)
            n = n >> 1
        return reversed_num
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The algorithm processes each of the 32 bits exactly once, shifting and masking to build the reversed bit pattern. It ensures correct 32-bit handling by iterating a fixed number of times. Strengths include simplicity and constant time/space. Weaknesses include no optimization for repeated calls (follow-up).
